title: Embedded Systems
shortSummary: Explore the computers inside robots. Embedded systems are the microcontrollers and single-board computers that read sensors, run control algorithms, and command actuators in real time.
whyItMatters: Every robot has a brain, and it is usually an embedded system — a small, dedicated computer designed to perform specific tasks with strict timing requirements. Unlike a desktop PC that runs many programs, an embedded system in a robot must read sensors, compute control outputs, and drive actuators hundreds or thousands of times per second without missing a beat. Understanding embedded systems means understanding the hardware-software interface where code meets the physical world. Arduino boards introduced millions of people to embedded programming, while platforms like the Raspberry Pi and NVIDIA Jetson enable increasingly powerful on-board intelligence. In Australia, field robots operating in remote areas must run embedded systems that are robust to temperature extremes, dust, and vibration, often with no network connectivity.
coreConcepts: Communication protocols: I2C, SPI, UART, CAN bus
Arduino, Raspberry Pi, and NVIDIA Jetson platforms
